title: Micranthes astilboides
intro: Micranthes astilboides, also known as the Western rockcress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the saxifrage family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Micranthes astilboides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs to support overall well-being and promote a healthy response to stress.
taxonomy: Micranthes astilboides is classified within the family Saxifragaceae, commonly known as the saxifrage family. This family is part of the larger order Saxifragales, which also includes other flowering plant families. Within the Saxifragaceae family, Micranthes astilboides is placed in the genus Micranthes, a group of flowering plants that are typically found in damp or moist environments. The genus Micranthes is further divided into several species, each with distinct characteristics and growth habits. Micranthes astilboides is one of these species, which is native to certain regions and is often found growing in rocky or shaded areas.
